text: Meeting deadlines is a crucial skill in both academic and professional settings. Effective time management helps you break large projects into smaller, manageable tasks with their own mini-deadlines. Prioritization is equally important, as it allows you to focus on urgent or high-impact work first. But sometimes, you may have too much on your plate. This is where scope management comes in: know when to say "no" or reduce unnecessary tasks to keep your workload realistic. Finally, communication is key. If you suspect a delay, inform your team or supervisor early. They may offer help or adjust the deadline, preventing last-minute stress.
